title: Chocolate Lava Cake
tagline: Classic molten lava cakes baked in a muffin mould with khaand and Amul butter — plus a quick protein mug-cake variation.
macroNotes: This is a treat-day dessert — mostly butter, chocolate and sugar, and protein-poor (~4 g/cake). Enjoy it as one, don't try to health-ify the classic version.
If you want the dessert to actually count as protein, make the protein mug-cake variation below (~25 g protein from a scoop of whey) instead of the classic.
Portion control is the real lever: one cake is ~315 kcal; two starts eating a big chunk of a 1600–1800 kcal day.

items: 6 tbsp (90 g) Amul butter (salted — so skip added salt)
1 tbsp cocoa powder, for dusting the moulds
(Skip vanilla — lava cake doesn't need it)
2 tsp sugar (important — protein + cocoa kill sweetness)
1 tsp melted butter or peanut butter
2 squares dark chocolate, for the centre

steps.title: Prep the moulds
steps.body: Grease 6 muffin cups with butter, dust with 1 tbsp cocoa powder and tap out the excess. Chill the tray in the fridge while you make the batter — a cold mould helps the lava centre.

steps.title: Melt chocolate + butter
steps.body: In a microwave-safe bowl, melt 1/2 cup chopped dark chocolate with 6 tbsp Amul butter in bursts: 30 sec → stir, 20 sec → stir, 20 sec → stir, 10–15 sec if needed. Stop as soon as it's smooth. Cool 2–3 minutes (warm, not hot). Do NOT melt the khaand in with it — raw sugar won't dissolve in fat and stays grainy.

steps.title: Powder the khaand
steps.body: If the khaand crystals are large, pulse 1/2 cup in a mixer for 10–15 seconds to semi-powdered (not full icing sugar) so the cake isn't gritty.

steps.title: Whisk eggs + khaand
steps.body: Whisk 2 eggs with the 1/2 cup powdered khaand for 30–45 seconds by hand — just until uniform, slightly thickened and foamy on top with no visible sugar grains. Don't whip in lots of air or you'll get a cakey texture instead of a molten centre. Since the butter is salted, add no extra salt.

steps.title: Combine & fold in flour
steps.body: Pour the warm (not hot) chocolate-butter into the egg mix and whisk gently 10–15 seconds. Add 1/4 cup maida and fold with a spatula, stopping the moment the dry flour disappears. The batter should be thick but pourable — like melted chocolate sauce, not pancake-thin or brownie-thick. No baking powder or baking soda for the classic version.

steps.title: Rest & fill
steps.body: Rest the batter 3–5 minutes (helps the khaand dissolve further). Take the mould from the fridge and fill each cup ~3/4 full. A chocolate square pushed into each centre is optional — with rich batter filled to ~3/4 you don't need it.

steps.title: Bake
steps.body: Bake in a preheated oven at 200°C (390°F), middle rack, for 9 minutes (8 min = very runny, 10 min = fudgy). Edges should look set and slightly pulled from the sides; centres soft and slightly shiny with a gentle jiggle. Don't go past 10 minutes on the first batch.

steps.title: Unmould
steps.body: Rest 1 minute, run a knife around the edges, and invert confidently onto a plate. Serve with vanilla ice cream or a dusting of sugar.

steps.title: Variation — protein mug cake
steps.body: For a high-protein single serving in a WIDE 300–350 ml microwave-safe mug: mix the dry ingredients (1 scoop chocolate whey, 1 tbsp cocoa, 1.5 tbsp maida, 2 tsp sugar, 1/4 tsp baking powder, pinch salt) thoroughly, then whisk in 1 egg, 2 tbsp milk and 1 tsp melted butter/PB until smooth. Pour half the batter in, add 2 chocolate squares, top with the rest (traps the molten centre). Microwave 30–40 seconds on HIGH — protein goes molten → overcooked in ~5 seconds, so err short. Stir the batter bottom-to-top before cooking so the base isn't undercooked, and don't skimp on the 2 tsp sugar or it tastes flat. Rest 10 seconds.

notes: Amul butter is salted — the slight sweet-salt contrast is actually nice in lava cake, so leave out any added salt.
Khaand makes the classic cake slightly more fudgy and less glossy than white sugar — that's normal; resting the batter and baking 9 (not 8) minutes helps the raw sugar cook through.
Leftover classic batter that won't fit an oven-safe dish: microwave it in a greased mug filled only halfway, in bursts (20 + 10 + 5–8 sec), never more than 45 seconds total, no lid.
For the protein mug cake, a wider mug beats a tall narrow one — it cooks more evenly and keeps the centre molten.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
